4887.question_en: M and N invest ₹80,000 and ₹1,20,000. After a year, the total profit, including 10% interest, is ₹33,000. What is N’s share?
4887.question_hi: M और N ने क्रमश: ₹80,000 और ₹1,20,000 का निवेश किया। एक वर्ष बाद, 10% ब्याज सहित कुल लाभ ₹33,000 है। N का हिस्सा क्या है?
4887.solution_en:
M's investment = ₹80,000

N's investment = ₹1,20,000

Investment ratio = 80,000 : 1,20,000 = 2 : 3

Total profit = ₹33,000

Profit-sharing ratio = 2 : 3

Total parts = 5

N's share = (3/5) × 33,000 = ₹19,800
4887.solution_hi:
M का निवेश = ₹80,000
N का निवेश = ₹1,20,000

निवेश अनुपात = 80,000 : 1,20,000= 2 : 3

कुल लाभ = ₹33,000

लाभ बाँटने का अनुपात = 2 : 3

कुल भाग = 5

N का हिस्सा= (3/5) × 33,000= ₹19,800

4887.question_en: The average age of a group of 8 people increases by 2 years when a person aged 14 is replaced by a new person. Find the age of the new person.
4887.question_hi: 8 लोगों के एक समूह की औसत आयु 2 वर्ष बढ़ जाती है जब 14 वर्ष की आयु वाले एक व्यक्ति को एक नए व्यक्ति से बदल दिया जाता है। नए व्यक्ति की आयु ज्ञात कीजिए।
4887.solution_en:
Total number of people in the group = 8

Increase in total age = 8 × 2 = 16 years

Age of the new person = 14 + 16 = 30 years
4887.solution_hi:
समूह में कुल लोग = 8

कुल आयु में वृद्धि= 8 × 2= 16 वर्ष

नई व्यक्ति की आयु= 14 + 16= 30 वर्ष

4887.question_en: The price of cooking oil increased by 8%. A family uses 12 litres of cooking oil per month. If the original price was ₹150 per litre, what is the additional annual expenditure (to the nearest Rs.) for the family due to the price hike?
4887.question_hi: खाना पकाने के तेल की कीमत में 8% की वृद्धि हुई। एक परिवार प्रति माह 12 लीटर खाना पकाने के तेल का उपयोग करता है। यदि मूल कीमत ₹150 प्रति लीटर थी, तो मूल्य वृद्धि के कारण परिवार के लिए अतिरिक्त वार्षिक व्यय (निकटतम रु. में) क्या है?
4887.solution_en:
Original price of oil = 150 per litre

Price increase = 8%

Increase per litre = 8% × 150 = 12

Family's monthly consumption = 12 litres

Additional monthly expenditure = 12 × 12 = 144

Additional annual expenditure = 144 × 12 = 1,728
4887.solution_hi:
तेल की मूल कीमत = 150 प्रति लीटर

मूल्य वृद्धि = 8%

प्रति लीटर वृद्धि= 8% × 150= 12

परिवार का मासिक उपयोग = 12 लीटर

अतिरिक्त मासिक व्यय= 12 × 12= 144

अतिरिक्त वार्षिक व्यय= 144 × 12=1,728

4887.question_en: A certain sum triples in 8 years under compound interest. How many years will it take for that sum to grow to nine times its original value?
4887.question_hi: चक्रवृद्धि ब्याज के तहत 8 वर्षों में एक निश्चित राशि तीन गुनी हो जाती है। उस राशि को अपने मूल मूल्य से नौ गुना बढ़ने में कितने वर्ष लगेंगे?
4887.solution_en:
Amount in 8 years = 3 times

9 times = 3 × 3

First 3 times → 8 years

Second 3 times → 8 years

Total time = 8 + 8 = 16 years
4887.solution_hi:
8 वर्षों में राशि = 3 गुना

9 गुना = 3 × 3

पहली बार 3 गुना → 8 वर्ष
दूसरी बार 3 गुना → 8 वर्ष

कुल समय =8 + 8 = 16 वर्ष

4887.question_en: A milkman bought 50 liters of milk for 2000. 5 liters of milk spoiled. At what rate per liter should he sell the remaining milk to make a profit of 20% on his total outlay?
4887.question_hi: एक दूधवाले ने 2000 में 50 लीटर दूध खरीदा। 5 लीटर दूध खराब हो गया। उसे शेष दूध को प्रति लीटर किस दर पर बेचना चाहिए कि उसे अपने कुल व्यय पर 20% का लाभ हो?
4887.option_en_1: 53.33 per liter
4887.option_en_2: 56.24 per liter
4887.option_en_3: 59.36 per liter
4887.option_en_4: 52.82 per liter
4887.option_hi_1: 53.33 प्रति लीटर
4887.option_hi_2: 56.24 प्रति लीटर
4887.option_hi_3: 59.36 प्रति लीटर
4887.option_hi_4: 52.82 प्रति लीटर
4887.solution_en:
Total cost = ₹2000

Selling price = 2000 + 20% of 2000

= 2000 + 400 = ₹2400

Remaining milk = 50 – 5 = 45 liters

Selling price per liter = 2400 ÷ 45 = 53.33
4887.solution_hi:
कुल व्यय = 2000

विक्रय मूल्य= 2000 + 20% of 2000

= 2000 + 400=2400

शेष दूध = 50 – 5 = 45 लीटर

प्रति लीटर विक्रय मूल्य= 2400 ÷ 45= 53.33

4887.question_en: A merchant mixes 60 kg of coffee at ₹90 /kg with a dearer variety at ₹120 /kg. He sells the blend at ₹110 /kg and gains 10 % profit. How many kg of the dearer variety did he add?
4887.question_hi: एक व्यापारी ₹90/किग्रा वाली 60 किग्रा कॉफी को ₹120/किग्रा वाली एक महंगी किस्म के साथ मिलाता है। वह मिश्रण को ₹110/किग्रा पर बेचता है और 10% लाभ कमाता है। उसने महंगी किस्म की कितनी किग्रा कॉफी मिलाई?
4887.option_hi_1: 30 किग्रा
4887.option_hi_2: 28 किग्रा
4887.option_hi_3: 15 किग्रा
4887.option_hi_4: 17 किग्रा
4887.solution_en:
Selling price = ₹110 per kg

Profit = 10%

Cost price = 110 × 100/110 = ₹100 per kg

₹90 variety : ₹120 variety

(120 − 100) : (100 − 90)

= 20 : 10

= 2 : 1

Cheaper : Costlier = 2 : 1

60 kg = 2 parts

1 part = 30 kg

Costlier coffee = 30 kg
4887.solution_hi:
विक्रय मूल्य = ₹110 प्रति किग्रा
लाभ = 10%

क्रय मूल्य= 110 × 100/110= ₹100 प्रति किग्रा

₹90 वाली : ₹120 वाली

(120 − 100) : (100 − 90)

= 20 : 10

= 2 : 1

सस्ती : महंगी = 2 : 1

60 किग्रा = 2 भाग

1 भाग = 30 किग्रा

महंगी कॉफी = 30 किग्रा

4887.question_en: A can complete a job in 36 days. Every third day, he is assisted by B who can complete the job alone in 48 days. In how many days will the job be completed?
4887.question_hi: A किसी काम को 36 दिनों में पूरा कर सकता है। हर तीसरे दिन, B उसकी सहायता करता है जो अकेले ही उस काम को 48 दिनों में पूरा कर सकता है। काम कितने दिनों में पूरा होगा?
4887.option_en_2: 25 1/7 days
4887.option_en_3: 29 1/7 days
4887.option_hi_1: 28 दिन
4887.option_hi_2: 25 1/7 दिन
4887.option_hi_3: 29 1/7 दिन
4887.option_hi_4: 35 दिन
4887.solution_en:
A's work in 1 day = 1/36

B's work in 1 day = 1/48

First day → A = 1/36

Second day → A = 1/36

Third day → A + B = 1/36 + 1/48

Total work -

= 1/36 + 1/36 + 1/36 + 1/48

= 3/36 + 1/48

= 1/12 + 1/48

= 5/48

Work done in 27 days (9 cycles) -

= 9 × 5/48

= 45/48

= 15/16

Remaining work = 1/16

Work done by A alone = (1/16) ÷ (1/36)

= 36/16 = 2 1/4 days

Total time = 27 + 2 1/4 ≈ 29 1/7 days
4887.solution_hi:
A का 1 दिन का काम = 1/36

B का 1 दिन का काम = 1/48

पहला दिन → A = 1/36
दूसरा दिन → A = 1/36
तीसरा दिन → A + B = 1/36 + 1/48

कुल काम-

= 1/36 + 1/36 + 1/36 + 1/48

= 3/36 + 1/48

= 1/12 + 1/48

= 5/48

27 दिन (9 चक्र) में काम -

= 9 × 5/48

= 45/48

= 15/16

शेष काम= 1/16

A अकेले करेगा = (1/16) ÷ (1/36)

= 36/16= 2 1/4 दिन

कुल समय= 27 + 2 ¼ ≈ 29 1/7 दिन

4887.question_en: A large ship consumes 0.2 liters of fuel per km, and a smaller ship consumes 0.15 liters per km. If the large ship travels 500 km and the small ship travels 400 km, what is the total fuel cost at a rate of ₹110 per liter?
4887.question_hi: एक बड़ा जहाज प्रति किमी 0.2 लीटर ईंधन की खपत करता है, और एक छोटा जहाज प्रति किमी 0.15 लीटर ईंधन की खपत करता है। यदि बड़ा जहाज 500 किमी और छोटा जहाज 400 किमी की यात्रा करता है, तो 110 प्रति लीटर की दर से कुल ईंधन लागत क्या है?
4887.solution_en:
Fuel for the large ship = 0.2 × 500 = 100 liters

Fuel for the small ship = 0.15 × 400 = 60 liters

Total fuel = 100 + 60 = 160 liters

Total cost = 160 × 110 = 17,600
4887.solution_hi:
बड़े जहाज का ईंधन= 0.2 × 500= 100 लीटर

छोटे जहाज का ईंधन= 0.15 × 400= 60 लीटर

कुल ईंधन= 100 + 60= 160 लीटर

कुल लागत= 160 × 110= 17,600

4887.question_en: If x + 1/(x + 1) = 6, then determine the value of (x + 2)³ + 1/(x + 2)³.
4887.question_hi: यदि x + 1/x+1 = 6, तो (x+ 2)3 + 1/(x+2)3 का मान निर्धारित करें ?
4887.solution_en:
x + 1/(x + 1) = 6

Let – y = x + 1

y + 1/y = 6

y² + 1 = 6y

y² − 6y + 1 = 0

y = 3 ± 2√2

y + 1 = 4 ± 2√2

a³ + b³ = (a + b)³ − 3ab(a + b)

a = y + 1

b = 1/(y + 1)

ab = 1

a + b = (y + 1) + 1/(y + 1)

= [(y + 1)² + 1]/(y + 1)

= (y² + 2y + 2)/(y + 1)

y² = 6y − 1

a + b = (6y − 1 + 2y + 2)/(y + 1)

= (8y + 1)/(y + 1)

Putting y = 3 + 2√2 -

a + b = 8

a³ + b³ = 8³ − 3×1×8

= 512 – 24 = 488
4887.solution_hi:
x + 1/(x + 1) = 6

माना - y = x + 1

y + 1/y = 6

y² + 1 = 6y

y² − 6y + 1 = 0

y = 3 ± 2√2

y + 1 = 4 ± 2√2

a³ + b³ = (a + b)³ − 3ab(a + b)

a = y + 1
b = 1/(y + 1)

ab = 1

a + b= (y + 1) + 1/(y + 1)

= [(y + 1)² + 1]/(y + 1)

= (y² + 2y + 2)/(y + 1)

y² = 6y − 1

a + b = (6y − 1 + 2y + 2)/(y + 1)

= (8y + 1)/(y + 1)

y = 3 + 2√2 रखने पर -

a + b = 8

a³ + b³ = 8³ − 3×1×8

= 512 – 24= 488

4887.question_en: You are faced with 5 doors. Behind one door is a car, and behind the other four are goats. You pick one door. The host, who knows where the car is, then opens 3 of the remaining doors, all revealing goats. You are then offered the chance to switch your choice to the one remaining unopened door. What is the probability of winning the car if you decide to switch?
4887.question_hi: आपके सामने 5 दरवाज़े हैं। एक दरवाज़े के पीछे एक कार है, और बाकी चार के पीछे बकरियाँ हैं। आप एक दरवाज़ा चुनें। मेज़बान, जो जानता है कि कार कहाँ है, फिर बचे हुए 3 दरवाज़े खोलता है, सभी में बकरियाँ दिखाई देती हैं। फिर आपको अपनी पसंद को एक बचे हुए बंद दरवाज़े पर बदलने का मौक़ा दिया जाता है। अगर आप बदलने का फ़ैसला करते हैं तो कार जीतने की संभावना क्या है?
4887.option_en_1: 1/2
4887.solution_en:
Probability that the car is behind the initially chosen door = 1/5

Total probability for the remaining 4 doors = 1 - 1/5 = 4/5

The host eliminates 3 of these 4 doors (those concealing goats), leaving only one unopened door.

The entire 4/5 probability associated with that group now shifts to this single remaining door.

Probability of winning by switching to this door = 4/5
4887.solution_hi:

4887.question_en: A hot-air balloon directly above point A is rising vertically at 2 m/s. An observer at point B, 500 m due east of A, watches the balloon. At the moment when the angle of elevation is 30°, how long until it reaches an angle of elevation of 60°? (Assume no horizontal drift.)

4887.question_en: A solid hemisphere is drilled with a cylindrical with radius R/2 hole through its center. If the height of the removed cylinder equals the hemisphere’s radius R, find the ratio of the volume removed to the volume remaining.
4887.question_hi: एक ठोस गोलार्ध को उसके केंद्र में एक बेलनाकार त्रिज्या R/2 छेद के साथ ड्रिल किया जाता है। यदि हटाए गए बेलन की ऊंचाई गोलार्ध की त्रिज्या R के बराबर है, तो हटाए गए आयतन का शेष आयतन से अनुपात ज्ञात कीजिए।
4887.solution_en:
Radius of the hemisphere = R

Radius of the cylindrical hole = R/2

Height of the removed cylinder = R

The removed part is considered to be only the cylinder.

Volume of the hemisphere = (2/3)πR³

Volume of the removed cylinder = πr²h

= π × (R/2)² × R

= (1/4)πR³

Remaining volume = (2/3 − 1/4)πR³

= (8 − 3)/12 πR³

= (5/12)πR³

= Removed : Remaining

= (1/4) : (5/12)

= 3 : 5
4887.solution_hi:
गोलार्ध की त्रिज्या = R

बेलनाकार छेद की त्रिज्या = R/2

हटाए गए बेलन की ऊँचाई = R

हटाया गया भाग केवल बेलन माना गया है।

गोलार्ध का आयतन= (2/3)πR³

हटाए गए बेलन का आयतन= πr²h

= π × (R/2)² × R

= (1/4)πR³

शेष आयतन= (2/3 − 1/4)πR³

= (8 − 3)/12 πR³

= (5/12)πR³

= हटाया गया : शेष

= (1/4) : (5/12)

= 3 : 5

4887.question_en: The base of a right prism is a regular hexagon with a side length of 8 cm. The height of the prism is 15 cm. Find the volume of the prism.

= 1440√3

≈ 2494 cm³

4887.question_en: Complement of an angle is 12° more than twice the angle. Find the angle.

4887.solution_en:
Angle = x°

Complementary angle = (90 − x)°

Complementary angle = 12° more than twice the angle

90 − x = 2x + 12

90 − 12 = 3x

78 = 3x

x = 26°
4887.solution_hi:

पूरक कोण = कोण के दोगुने से 12° अधिक

90 − 12 = 3x

78 = 3x

x = 26°

4887.question_en: In triangle ABC, the coordinates of vertices A and B are (0,0) and (6,0), respectively. Vertex C lies somewhere on the line y =x, and the centroid of triangle ABC is (3,5).What are the coordinates of point C?

Centroid formula = (x1 +x2 x3) ÷ 3 , (y1 + y2 +y3) ÷ 3

(0 + 6 +a) ÷ 3 = 5

6 + a = 15

a = 9

c = (a,a) = (9 , 9)

4887.question_en: What is the slope of the line perpendicular to 7x + 4y = 6 ?

4887.question_en: If mean, median, and mode of a moderately skewed observations are (x - 1), (2x - 4) and '2x', respectively, then find the value of 'x'.
4887.question_hi: अगर एक मध्यम विकृत प्रेक्षण का माध्य, माध्यिका और बहुलक क्रमश: (x - 1), (2x - 4) और '2x' हैं, तो 'x' का मान ज्ञात कीजिए।
4887.solution_en:
Mode = 3 × Median − 2 × Mean

Mean = (x − 1)

Median = (2x − 4)

mode = 2x

2x = 3(2x − 4) − 2(x − 1)

2x = 6x − 12 − 2x + 2

2x = 4x − 10

10 = 2x

x = 5
4887.solution_hi:

माध्य = (x − 1)
माध्यिका = (2x − 4)
बहुलक = 2x

2x = 3(2x − 4) − 2(x − 1)

2x = 6x − 12 − 2x + 2

2x = 4x − 10

10 = 2x

x = 5

4974.question_en:
Read the passage and answer the questions:
"The truth of the self-conscious individual lies not in its immediate certainty, but in the dialectical movement of its becoming. Consciousness, in its pursuit of knowledge, initially assumes that its object is something external and independent. Yet, through the labor of experience, it discovers that this object is not truly separate but is mediated by consciousness itself. The apparent duality of subject and object is thus sublated in the dialectic, revealing that knowledge is not the passive reception of an external truth but an active process in which consciousness shapes and transforms its own reality. This movement unfolds through a series of contradictions, in which each stage of consciousness, believing itself to have attained certainty, encounters its own inadequacy. The master-slave dialectic exemplifies this: self-consciousness seeks recognition from another, yet in asserting its dominance over the other, it paradoxically undermines its own freedom. The master, in reducing the slave to a mere instrument, deprives himself of a fully realized recognition. Meanwhile, the slave, through labor and self-transformation, attains a deeper awareness of autonomy, demonstrating that freedom is not given but achieved through negation and overcoming.
Which of the following best identifies the grammatical role of the phrase "in its pursuit of knowledge" in the sentence:

"Consciousness, in its pursuit of knowledge, initially assumes that its object is something external and independent."

4974.option_en_2: Adverbial phrase modifying "assumes"
4974.option_en_3: Noun phrase functioning as the subject
4974.option_hi_2: Adverbial phrase modifying "assumes"
4974.option_hi_3: Noun phrase functioning as the subject
4974.solution_en:
Correct Answer

Adverbial phrase modifying "assumes"

Explanation

In the sentence, the phrase "in its pursuit of knowledge" functions as an adverbial phrase of purpose/condition. It answers the question of why or when consciousness performs the action of the main verb. Specifically, it tells us the condition under which consciousness "assumes" that its object is external.

Because it provides context, timing, or motive for the action of the verb rather than describing a noun, its grammatical role is to modify the verb "assumes".

Key Points

Target Phrase: "...in its pursuit of knowledge..."

Grammatical Function: Adverbial modifier.

Why other options fail: * It is not an adjective because it doesn't describe the static nature of the noun "Consciousness."

It is not a noun phrase functioning as the subject because "Consciousness" is already the subject.

It is not an appositive explaining "object" because it sits far away from the word "object" and doesn't rename or redefine it.

"The truth of the self-conscious individual lies not in its immediate certainty, but in the dialectical movement of its becoming. Meanwhile, the slave, through labor and self-transformation, attains a deeper awareness of autonomy, demonstrating that freedom is not given but achieved through negation and overcoming.

What grammatical structure is "believing" a part of?

4974.question_en: What term describes: "The view that all human knowledge and experience are shaped by the limitations of the human mind, and thus we cannot know things as they truly are."
4974.question_hi: What term describes: "The view that all human knowledge and experience are shaped by the limitations of the human mind, and thus we cannot know things as they truly are."
4974.solution_en:
Correct Answer

Kantianism

Explanation

The view described is a core tenet of Kantianism, established by philosopher Immanuel Kant. Kant argued that human knowledge is structured by the innate faculties and limitations of the human mind (such as our perception of space and time). Because our minds actively process and shape our experiences, we can only know things as they appear to us (phenomena), and we cannot know things as they truly are in themselves (noumena).

Key Points

Definition: The philosophy that knowledge is shaped by the human mind's limitations, separating appearances from the true nature of things.

Philosophical Basis: Immanuel Kant's distinction between the phenomenal world (things as perceived) and the noumenal world (things in themselves).

4974.question_en: What is the term for: "A literary or rhetorical device in which the opposite of what is expected occurs"?
4974.question_hi: What is the term for: "A literary or rhetorical device in which the opposite of what is expected occurs"?
4974.solution_en:
Correct Answer

Situational Irony

Explanation

Situational Irony is a literary and rhetorical device that occurs when there is a striking contradiction between what is reasonably expected to happen and what actually takes place.

While verbal irony involves a contradiction between what is said and what is meant, situational irony centers entirely on an unexpected twist in events or circumstances (e.g., a fire station burning down).

Key Points

Definition: A discrepancy between expected results and actual outcomes.

Example: A marriage counselor filing for divorce.

4974.question_en:
Fill in the blank with the most appropriate option:
Of all the analysts on the panel, Priya was considered the ___ in macroeconomic interpretation.
4974.question_hi:
Fill in the blank with the most appropriate option:
Of all the analysts on the panel, Priya was considered the ___ in macroeconomic interpretation.
4974.solution_en:
Correct Answer

best

Explanation

The sentence opens with the phrase "Of all the analysts on the panel", which indicates that a comparison is being made among three or more entities (a whole group). When comparing more than two things, the superlative degree of the adjective must be used.

The superlative form of "good" is "best".

Key Points

Grammar Rule: Use comparative adjectives (better) for comparing two things, and superlative adjectives (best) for comparing three or more things.

Double Superlatives: Phrases like "most better" or "more best" are grammatically incorrect double modifiers and should always be avoided.

4974.question_en: A sentence is provided in direct speech. From the four given options, choose the one that most accurately conveys the sentence in its corresponding indirect speech. "How strange that nobody noticed the missing file until today!" exclaimed the clerk.

4974.solution_en:
Correct Answer

The clerk exclaimed that it was strange that no one had noticed the missing file until that day.

Explanation

When converting an exclamatory sentence from direct speech to indirect speech, several specific grammatical transformations must take place:

Reporting Verb: The phrase "exclaimed the clerk" is accurately carried over as "The clerk exclaimed that...".

Tense Change (Simple Past to Past Perfect): The direct speech contains the simple past verb "noticed". In indirect speech, this must backshift to the past perfect tense: "had noticed".

Time/Place Expression Change: The word "today" in direct speech must be updated to "that day" in indirect speech to reflect the shift in perspective.

Option 2 correctly implements all of these mandatory rules.

Key Points

Tense Shift:noticed (Simple Past) → had noticed (Past Perfect)

Time Shift: today → that day

4974.question_en:
Rearrange the following sentences in correct order to make a meaningful sentence
P: while the committee deliberated on the proposed reforms,
Q: numerous stakeholders voiced their concerns
R: regarding the potential impacts
S: during the public consultation process
4974.question_hi:
Rearrange the following sentences in correct order to make a meaningful sentence
P: while the committee deliberated on the proposed reforms,
Q: numerous stakeholders voiced their concerns
R: regarding the potential impacts
S: during the public consultation process
4974.solution_en:
Correct Answer

QPRS

Explanation

To arrange the fragments into a meaningful sentence, we look at the logical flow of the main subject, action, and modifiers:

Q (Numerous stakeholders voiced their concerns) acts as the primary independent clause, giving us a clear subject (stakeholders) and verb (voiced).

P (while the committee deliberated on the proposed reforms,) is a subordinate time clause that sets up a parallel background action.

R (regarding the potential impacts) is a prepositional phrase modifying the noun concerns, clarifying what those concerns were about.

S (during the public consultation process) provides the final context of when or where this entire interaction happened.

Putting it all together: "Numerous stakeholders voiced their concerns while the committee deliberated on the proposed reforms, regarding the potential impacts during the public consultation process."

Key Points

Sequence: Q-P-R-S

Structural Tip: Identify the main independent clause first (Q) to anchor the rest of your modifiers.

Step 4 (Sentence 1):The team submitted the compiled data... — Finally, after it is fully processed and compiled, the clean data is sent off to the analytics division.
